title: AI can generate new, useful ideas and solutions
description: An AI system can produce novel hypotheses, designs, and solution paths that are genuinely useful rather than generic rearrangements of familiar patterns.

subQuestions.title: Can it propose novel hypotheses, designs, or solution paths?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system generates options that move beyond obvious summaries or recombinations.
subQuestions.rationale: Systems can generate many plausible new directions, but the ratio of truly valuable ideas to generic ones is still uneven.

proofItems.title: Expert blind review: Novel hypothesis and solution proposal quality
proofItems.source: Expert Review Panel on AI Performance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Expert blind review found that models generate many plausible ideas, but only a minority are judged meaningfully non-obvious and worth pursuing.

subQuestions.title: Can experts judge its outputs as non-trivial?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether expert raters distinguish the output as meaningfully valuable rather than boilerplate.
subQuestions.rationale: Experts do sometimes rate outputs as non-trivial, but not consistently enough to call this solved.

proofItems.title: Expert blind review: Expert judgment of non-trivial output quality
proofItems.source: Expert Review Panel on AI Performance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Panel reviews show some outputs standing out as genuinely useful, but many still read as polished synthesis rather than strong original contribution.

subQuestions.title: Can it outperform baseline ideation workflows?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether using the system creates better or broader idea portfolios than ordinary baseline workflows.
subQuestions.rationale: In many ideation settings, models already improve breadth and speed relative to ordinary first-pass brainstorming workflows.

proofItems.title: Controlled study: AI-assisted ideation versus baseline workflows
proofItems.shortExplanation: Controlled comparisons show AI-assisted teams producing broader option sets faster than baseline ideation processes.

subQuestions.title: Can it adapt novelty to concrete constraints and goals?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether creative output remains useful when cost, timing, audience, or technical constraints are explicit.
subQuestions.rationale: Constraint-aware creativity is improving, but models still swing between generic novelty and impractical overreach.

proofItems.title: Controlled study: Constraint-aware creative generation
proofItems.shortExplanation: Evaluation studies show useful creativity under moderate constraints, but weaker performance when constraints become numerous or tightly coupled.

subQuestions.title: Can it distinguish useful synthesis from generic boilerplate?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system can identify and avoid outputs that only sound polished without adding real value.
subQuestions.rationale: Systems can sometimes critique generic output well, but still frequently produce or accept it in practice.

proofItems.title: Expert blind review: Detection of generic versus genuinely useful synthesis
proofItems.source: Expert Review Panel on AI Performance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Reviewer studies show partial success in distinguishing empty polish from useful synthesis, but still document over-acceptance of generic output.
